growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: BVMN (Buffered/modified Vincent's minimal medium + 10 mM nitrate), Cu-standard (0.02 uM Cu)

up.method: 6.6 mM consumed over 4 days (colorimetric Nitrate assay)

sec.method: transiently accumulated ~1.5 mM then fully consumed by day 6 (colorimetric)

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: microaerobic growth under Cu-L conditions decreased compared with that reached under Cu-S conditions (about 0.4 and 0.6 OD600...). while 8.7 mM NO3- was consumed by cells grown under Cu-H conditions, only 6.6 mM NO3- was taken by Cu-S grown
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 microoxic (2% O2) Cu-S BVMN, 30C, pH6.8, 170rpm. Reached OD600 ~0.6 at 7 d (no exponential mu reported). Nitrate consumption: 6.6 mM/4 d -> 6.6/(4x24)=0.0688 mM/h volumetric average (full 10 mM by day5 -> 10/120=0.0833 mM/h). NOT normalized to gDW (only OD given). NO2- transiently ~1.5 mM, consumed from day3 to zero by day6. N2OR (MV-dependent) activity 74.10 nmol N2O consumed/(mg protein h) for WT Cu-S (in-vitro enzyme activity, not physiological flux).

sec.method: NO2- not detected throughout incubation (high NirK activity)

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: cells grown in the Cu-H medium showed a significant increase in growth rates... (about 0.7 and 0.6 OD600...). while 8.7 mM NO3- was consumed by cells grown under Cu-H conditions... after 4 days... N2O was not detected in the headspace of Cu
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 microoxic Cu-H (13 uM) BVMN, 30C pH6.8. Highest microaerobic growth (OD600 ~0.7 at 7d). Nitrate uptake 8.7 mM/4 d -> 8.7/96=0.0906 mM/h volumetric average (not per gDW). Full denitrification: NO2- and N2O both undetectable (complete reduction to N2). No exponential mu reported.

growth_records.medium: BVMN, Cu-limited (Cu-L; Cu omitted + 10 uM BCS chelator + 1 mM ascorbate)

up.method: only ~7 mM of 10 mM consumed over 7 days (incomplete; colorimetric)

sec.method: accumulated 1.5 mM NO2- from day 1, not further consumed over 7 days (NirK-limited)

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: only 7 mM NO3- was taken from the medium... NO2- accumulated in the Cu-L medium (1.5 mM NO2-) that was not further consumed... Under Cu-L conditions, N2O was accumulated from the third day of incubation, increasing its concentration from 16
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 microoxic Cu-L BVMN, 30C pH6.8. Reduced growth (OD600 ~0.4 at 7d). Nitrate uptake incomplete ~7 mM over 7 d -> 7/(7x24)=0.0417 mM/h upper-bound volumetric avg (Nap partially inhibited by NO2-). NO2- accumulates 1.5 mM (NirK depressed under Cu limitation), not consumed. N2O accumulates (Nos inactive w/o Cu): (118.12-16.61)/(4x24)=1.057 uM/h headspace over d3-d7. All volumetric, not gDW-normalized.

up.method: NO3- required for anaerobic growth; consumption not separately quantified for anoxic condition

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: Cu-S and Cu-H cultures reached an OD600 of about 0.5, indicating that growth was severely affected in the Cu-L medium... Cu and NO3- were both indispensable for NO3- respiration under anoxic conditions.
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 anoxic Cu-S BVMN, 30C pH6.8, nitrate sole e-acceptor. OD600 ~0.5 at 7d. No mu or quantitative NO3- rate reported for the anoxic condition (rates quantified only microoxically). Record kept for growth-condition completeness.

up.method: limited NO3- respiration under Cu-L; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: Cu-L cultures reached a turbidity (OD600) of about 0.2 after 7 days of incubation... indicating that growth was severely affected in the Cu-L medium compared with Cu-S or Cu-H media. Cu was essential for nitrate-dependent anaerobic growth.
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 anoxic Cu-L BVMN, 30C pH6.8. OD600 only ~0.2 at 7d (severe Cu limitation blocks NO3- respiration, growth ~ that without nitrate). No mu or quantitative rate reported.

up.method: aerobic growth by O2 respiration; rate not quantified

growth_records.cite: Pacheco, Pedro J. et al. Effect of Copper on Expression of Functional Genes and Proteins Associated with Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens Denitrification. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2022). DOI:10.3390/ijms23063386. [PMC8951191]
growth_records.snippet: Under oxic conditions, cultures reached an optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of around 1.5 after 7 days of incubation, regardless of the Cu treatment... Cu was not a limiting factor for B. diazoefficiens growth by oxygen respiration.
growth_records.notes: B. diazoefficiens 110spc4 oxic Cu-S BVMN, 30C pH6.8, arabinose C-source. OD600 ~1.5 at 7d, Cu-independent (Cu-independent bd-type oxidases). No exponential mu or substrate uptake rate reported. Reference aerobic growth condition.
